title: Rollbar API access tokens should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Rollbar API access token detected in source code. Rollbar is an error monitoring and debugging platform that aggregates and analyzes errors, exceptions, and logs from applications. These API access tokens authenticate programmatic access to Rollbar's API, granting the ability to send error reports (post_server_item, post_client_item tokens), read project data, manage incidents, configure alerts, access source maps, retrieve deploy information, and modify project settings. Exposing this token allows attackers to send fake error reports flooding your monitoring system with false alerts causing alert fatigue and obscuring real issues, access sensitive error data including stack traces with intellectual property and application architecture details, view user data captured in error contexts potentially violating privacy regulations, exfiltrate production error logs containing sensitive information like API keys or tokens embedded in error messages, manipulate incident resolution status disrupting on-call workflows and SLA tracking, delete or modify historical error data destroying audit trails and compliance records, create malicious integrations to exfiltrate data to external systems, access source maps revealing original unminified code and business logic, consume API quota causing legitimate error reporting to fail resulting in blind spots during critical incidents, and potentially access deploy history and configuration revealing deployment patterns and infrastructure details. IMMEDIATELY revoke this token from your Rollbar account settings at https://rollbar.com/settings/access_tokens/ (navigate to Account Settings → Project Access Tokens or Account Access Tokens), generate a new replacement token with appropriate scopes and permissions (use read-only tokens for non-critical operations, post_client_item for browser errors, post_server_item for server errors), audit recent API activity in Rollbar dashboard for unauthorized error submissions or suspicious API calls, review recent error reports for injected fake errors or data exfiltration attempts, check incident management workflow for unauthorized status changes, inspect integrations for malicious webhooks or external connections, review source map access logs if using Rollbar's source map feature, store tokens securely using environment variables, secret management systems (AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault, HashiCorp Vault, GCP Secret Manager), or encrypted configuration files (never commit tokens to version control or expose in client-side code), implement token rotation every 90 days as a security best practice, use separate tokens for different environments (development, staging, production) to limit blast radius of compromise, apply principle of least privilege when creating tokens (use read-only or post-only tokens instead of full access where possible), implement rate limiting in your error reporting to prevent abuse if token is compromised, monitor Rollbar usage patterns and set up alerts for anomalous activity (sudden spike in error reports, API calls from unexpected IPs), ensure error reports do not capture sensitive data (sanitize PII, credentials, API keys before sending to Rollbar), and comply with data retention and privacy regulations when storing error logs containing user data.
